titleStmt.title: Letter from the Resident Medical Superintendent of Kilkenny to William Richard Dawson, 21 June 1916
Letters 1916-1923

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A letter from the Resident Medical Superintendent of Kilkenny to William Richard Dawson (1864-1950), the Inspector of Lunatics. William Richard Dawson was appointed HM inspector of lunatics in Ireland in 1911. During the Great War he served in the Royal Ambulance Medical Corps, gaining the rank of lieutenant-colonel, as a specialist in ‘nerve disease’ or shell shock to the troops in Ireland.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary’s Office Registered papers, CSO-RP-1916-5615-1119-2

correspAction.persName: William Richard Dawson, Inspector of Lunatics

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
277 District Asylum, Kilkenny 29 November, 1915 21 st 20t day of January 1916 Gentlemen, I beg to inform you at their meeting
on the 18 th inst. the Committee adopted a set
of Rules for the management of the Asylum
which when they are properly revised will be
sent on to you in a few days for your
consideration and for their sanction is deemed
suitable. I am
Gentlemen,
Your obedient Servant, Locus Rn GGI
The Inspector of Lunatics
Dublin Castle
